_meta.schema: Each chronology is {title, subtitle, source, icon, color, era, entries: [...]}. Each entry: {name, verseRef, refs?, dates?, regnal?, age?, lifespan?, bornTo?, notes?, alias?}. Dates are scholarly conventions (Thiele for kings, traditional Masoretic for patriarchs).

entries.name: Adam
entries.notes: First man · created by God on day six (Gen 1:27) · died in year 930 from creation.

entries.name: Seth
entries.notes: Born when Adam was 130 (Gen 5:3) · ancestor of Noah.

entries.name: Enos
entries.notes: In his time "men began to call upon the name of the LORD" (Gen 4:26).

entries.name: Jared
entries.notes: Father of Enoch.

entries.name: Enoch
entries.notes: Walked with God · was translated without dying (Gen 5:24; Heb 11:5) · prophesied (Jude 14).

entries.name: Methuselah
entries.notes: Longest-lived person in Scripture · died the year of the Flood.

entries.name: Lamech
entries.notes: Father of Noah · prophesied comfort through Noah (Gen 5:29).

entries.name: Noah
entries.notes: Built the ark · entered at age 600 · lived 350 years after the Flood.

entries.name: Shem
entries.notes: Eldest son of Noah · ancestor of Abraham.

entries.name: Arphaxad
entries.notes: Born two years after the Flood.

entries.name: Eber
entries.notes: Eponymous ancestor of "Hebrew".

entries.name: Peleg
entries.notes: "In his days the earth was divided" (Gen 10:25).

entries.name: Nahor
entries.notes: Grandfather of Abraham (his namesake grandson is Abraham's brother).

entries.name: Terah
entries.notes: Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran · died in Haran.

entries.name: Abram
entries.notes: Renamed Abraham (Gen 17:5) · called from Ur · father of the faithful.

patriarchs_genesis.title: Patriarchs of Israel
patriarchs_genesis.subtitle: Abraham → Joseph · the founding family · Genesis 12–50

entries.name: Abraham
entries.notes: Born ~2166 BC (traditional) · called at 75 (Gen 12:4) · Isaac born when he was 100 (Gen 21:5).

entries.name: Sarah
entries.notes: Wife of Abraham · only woman in Scripture whose age at death is recorded.

entries.name: Isaac
entries.notes: Son of promise · married Rebekah at 40 (Gen 25:20) · twin sons born when he was 60 (Gen 25:26).

entries.name: Jacob
entries.notes: Renamed Israel (Gen 32:28) · father of the twelve tribes · died in Egypt.

entries.name: Joseph
entries.notes: Sold into Egypt at 17 · viceroy of Egypt · died in Egypt; bones taken back to Canaan (Ex 13:19).

judges.title: Judges of Israel
judges.subtitle: Othniel → Samuel · between Joshua and Saul · Book of Judges + 1 Samuel

entries.name: Othniel
entries.notes: Nephew of Caleb · delivered Israel from Cushan-rishathaim of Mesopotamia.

entries.name: Ehud
entries.notes: Left-handed Benjamite · slew Eglon king of Moab.

entries.name: Shamgar
entries.notes: Slew 600 Philistines with an oxgoad · also mentioned Judg 5:6.

entries.name: Deborah & Barak
entries.notes: Prophetess Deborah judged from under a palm in Ephraim · Barak defeated Sisera.

entries.name: Gideon
entries.notes: 300-man victory over Midian (Judg 7) · refused kingship (Judg 8:23).

entries.name: Abimelech
entries.notes: Son of Gideon by a concubine · killed his 70 brothers and ruled Shechem · died ignominiously by a millstone.

entries.name: Tola
entries.notes: From the tribe of Issachar.

entries.name: Jair
entries.notes: Gileadite · had 30 sons riding 30 ass colts.

entries.name: Jephthah
entries.notes: Gileadite outcast · vow led to sacrifice of his daughter (Judg 11:30-40).

entries.name: Ibzan
entries.notes: From Bethlehem · 30 sons + 30 daughters.

entries.name: Elon
entries.notes: Of the tribe of Zebulun.

entries.name: Abdon
entries.notes: From Pirathon in Ephraim · 40 sons + 30 grandsons riding ass colts.

entries.name: Samson
entries.notes: Nazarite from birth · slew 1,000 Philistines with a jawbone · final temple-collapse at Gaza.

entries.name: Eli
entries.notes: High priest at Shiloh · judged Israel · died on hearing of the ark's capture.

entries.name: Samuel
entries.notes: Last judge · prophet · anointed Saul and David.

kings_united.title: Kings of United Israel
kings_united.subtitle: Saul · David · Solomon · ~1050–931 BC · 1 & 2 Samuel, 1 Kings 1–11

entries.name: Saul
entries.notes: Of the tribe of Benjamin · first king of Israel · rejected by God · died at Mount Gilboa (1 Sam 31).

entries.name: David
entries.notes: From Bethlehem · reigned 7.5 years over Judah at Hebron, then 33 years over all Israel from Jerusalem · ancestor of Christ.

entries.name: Solomon
entries.notes: Built the Temple (1 Kgs 6) · author of Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Solomon · 700 wives + 300 concubines.

kings_judah.title: Kings of Judah
kings_judah.subtitle: Rehoboam → Zedekiah · the southern kingdom · 931–586 BC · 1 & 2 Kings, 2 Chronicles

entries.name: Rehoboam
entries.notes: Son of Solomon · refused the elders' advice; ten tribes seceded (1 Kgs 12).

entries.name: Asa
entries.notes: Reformer · removed idols · diseased in his feet (1 Kgs 15:23).

entries.name: Jehoshaphat
entries.notes: Allied with Ahab · godly king · sent teachers throughout Judah (2 Chr 17:7-9).

entries.name: Jehoram
entries.notes: Married Athaliah of the house of Ahab · died of a bowel disease (2 Chr 21:18-19).

entries.name: Ahaziah
entries.notes: Killed by Jehu (2 Kgs 9:27).

entries.name: Athaliah
entries.notes: Queen mother who usurped the throne · only female ruler of Judah · executed in coup.

entries.name: Joash
entries.notes: Crowned at age 7 · hidden by Jehosheba (2 Kgs 11:2-3) · repaired the Temple.

entries.name: Amaziah
entries.notes: Slew Edomites at the Valley of Salt · slain at Lachish (2 Kgs 14:19).

entries.name: Uzziah
entries.notes: Strong king · struck with leprosy for usurping priestly duties (2 Chr 26:16-21) · Isaiah's call in his death-year (Isa 6:1).

entries.name: Jotham
entries.notes: Co-regent during Uzziah's leprosy.

entries.name: Ahaz
entries.notes: Sacrificed his son in the fire (2 Kgs 16:3) · Isaiah delivered the Immanuel prophecy to him (Isa 7).

entries.name: Hezekiah
entries.notes: Major reformer · destroyed the brazen serpent (2 Kgs 18:4) · life extended 15 years (2 Kgs 20:6) · withstood Sennacherib (2 Kgs 19).

entries.name: Manasseh
entries.notes: Longest reign in Judah · most wicked · later repented in Babylonian captivity (2 Chr 33:11-13).

entries.name: Amon
entries.notes: Assassinated by his servants (2 Kgs 21:23).

entries.name: Josiah
entries.notes: Crowned at 8 · greatest reformer · Book of the Law rediscovered (2 Kgs 22:8) · slain at Megiddo by Pharaoh-necho.

entries.name: Jehoahaz
entries.notes: Deposed by Pharaoh-necho · died in Egypt (2 Kgs 23:34).

entries.name: Jehoiakim
entries.notes: Set up by Pharaoh-necho · burned Jeremiah's scroll (Jer 36:23) · vassal of Babylon.

entries.name: Jehoiachin
entries.notes: Carried captive to Babylon · later released by Evil-merodach (2 Kgs 25:27).

entries.name: Zedekiah
entries.notes: Last king of Judah · saw his sons killed before his eyes were put out · taken in chains to Babylon (2 Kgs 25:7) · Jerusalem destroyed.

kings_israel.title: Kings of Northern Israel
kings_israel.subtitle: Jeroboam I → Hoshea · the breakaway northern kingdom · 931–722 BC

entries.name: Jeroboam I
entries.notes: Set up golden calves at Bethel and Dan · the archetypal evil king ("sin of Jeroboam").

entries.name: Nadab
entries.notes: Killed by Baasha at Gibbethon.

entries.name: Baasha
entries.notes: Wiped out the house of Jeroboam · prophesied against by Jehu the seer (1 Kgs 16:1-4).

entries.name: Elah
entries.notes: Killed while drunk by his servant Zimri (1 Kgs 16:9-10).

entries.name: Zimri
entries.notes: Burned the palace over himself when Omri besieged Tirzah.

entries.name: Omri
entries.notes: Built Samaria as the new capital · founder of a long-lived dynasty · references in Assyrian and Moabite inscriptions.

entries.name: Ahab
entries.notes: Married Jezebel of Sidon · battled Elijah · died at Ramoth-gilead by a random arrow (1 Kgs 22:34).

entries.name: Ahaziah
entries.notes: Fell through a lattice · sent to Baal-zebub for healing · died per Elijah's word (2 Kgs 1).

entries.name: Jehoram
entries.notes: Removed Baal pillar · killed by Jehu (2 Kgs 9:24).

entries.name: Jehu
entries.notes: Anointed by Elisha's servant · annihilated the house of Ahab and the priests of Baal · pictured bowing to Shalmaneser III on the Black Obelisk.

entries.name: Jehoahaz
entries.notes: Reign weakened by Hazael of Syria.

entries.name: Jehoash
entries.notes: Visited the dying Elisha (2 Kgs 13:14).

entries.name: Jeroboam II
entries.notes: Restored Israel's borders · prosperity that Amos and Hosea decried.

entries.name: Zechariah
entries.notes: Last of Jehu's dynasty · slain by Shallum.

entries.name: Shallum
entries.notes: Killed by Menahem after one month.

entries.name: Menahem
entries.notes: Paid 1,000 talents of silver to Tiglath-pileser of Assyria (2 Kgs 15:19-20).

entries.name: Pekahiah
entries.notes: Slain by his captain Pekah.

entries.name: Pekah
entries.notes: Tiglath-pileser deported the trans-Jordan tribes during his reign (2 Kgs 15:29).

entries.name: Hoshea
entries.notes: Last king of Israel · Samaria fell to Shalmaneser V/Sargon II in 722 BC · ten tribes carried into exile.

pharaohs.title: Pharaohs of Egypt
pharaohs.subtitle: Often unnamed in Scripture; identifications follow scholarly consensus

entries.name: Pharaoh of Abraham's time
entries.notes: Took Sarai into his house · plagued by God; possibly an Old Kingdom or early Middle Kingdom ruler. Unnamed.

entries.name: Pharaoh of Joseph
entries.notes: Promoted Joseph to second-in-command · likely a Hyksos ruler or 12th-dynasty king. Unnamed.

entries.name: Pharaoh "who knew not Joseph"
entries.dates: c. 1450 BC (early date) or 1290 BC (late date)
entries.notes: Began the oppression of Israel · ordered the Hebrew male infants killed. Often identified with Thutmose III or Seti I. Unnamed.

entries.name: Pharaoh of the Exodus
entries.notes: Hardened heart through ten plagues · perished at the Red Sea. Often identified as Amenhotep II (early) or Ramesses II (late). Unnamed in Scripture.

entries.name: Shishak
entries.notes: Founder of the 22nd dynasty · invaded Judah in Rehoboam's 5th year and looted the Temple.

entries.name: Zerah the Ethiopian
entries.notes: Came against Asa with a million-man army · routed at Mareshah.

entries.name: So
entries.notes: Hoshea sent messengers to him for help · likely Osorkon IV or general Sib'e.

entries.name: Tirhakah
entries.notes: 25th-dynasty Cushite pharaoh · marched against Sennacherib in Hezekiah's day.

entries.name: Pharaoh-necho
entries.notes: Killed Josiah at Megiddo · deposed Jehoahaz · defeated by Nebuchadnezzar at Carchemish (605 BC).

entries.name: Pharaoh-hophra
entries.notes: Egyptian ally Zedekiah relied on against Babylon · prophesied to fall by Jeremiah.

assyrian_kings.title: Kings of Assyria
assyrian_kings.subtitle: The empire that destroyed northern Israel

entries.name: Tiglath-pileser III
entries.notes: Took tribute from Menahem · deported the trans-Jordan tribes.

entries.name: Shalmaneser V
entries.notes: Besieged Samaria for three years · died during the siege.

entries.name: Sargon II
entries.notes: Completed the conquest of Samaria and the deportation of the ten tribes.

entries.name: Sennacherib
entries.notes: Invaded Judah in Hezekiah's 14th year · 185,000 men slain by the angel of the LORD outside Jerusalem · murdered by his sons in Nineveh.

entries.name: Esarhaddon
entries.notes: Conquered Egypt · resettled foreign peoples in Samaria.

babylonian_kings.title: Kings of Babylon
babylonian_kings.subtitle: The empire that destroyed Jerusalem and exiled Judah

entries.name: Merodach-baladan
entries.notes: Sent envoys to Hezekiah after his recovery · Isaiah prophesied the future Babylonian captivity in response.

entries.name: Nabopolassar
entries.notes: Founded the Neo-Babylonian Empire · destroyed Nineveh in 612 BC. Not named in Scripture but father of Nebuchadnezzar.

entries.name: Nebuchadnezzar II
entries.notes: Defeated Pharaoh-necho at Carchemish · destroyed Jerusalem in 586 BC · driven mad seven years (Dan 4) · restored to acknowledge "the Most High".

entries.name: Evil-merodach
entries.notes: Released Jehoiachin from prison in his accession year.

entries.name: Belshazzar
entries.notes: Son of Nabonidus · saw the writing on the wall · slain the night Babylon fell to the Medes and Persians.

persian_kings.title: Kings of Persia
persian_kings.subtitle: Cyrus → Artaxerxes · the empire that ended the exile

entries.name: Cyrus the Great
entries.notes: Conquered Babylon in 539 BC · issued the decree releasing the Jews to return and rebuild the Temple · named in Isaiah 200 years before his birth.

entries.name: Darius the Mede
entries.notes: Took the kingdom when Belshazzar fell · cast Daniel into the lions' den · sometimes identified with Cyaxares II or Gubaru.

entries.name: Cambyses II
entries.notes: Cyrus's son · conquered Egypt · not directly named in Scripture but reigned during the temple-building delays of Ezra 4.

entries.name: Darius I
entries.notes: Re-issued Cyrus's decree · the Temple was completed in his 6th year (516 BC) · Haggai and Zechariah prophesied during his reign.

entries.name: Ahasuerus
entries.notes: Persian king of the Esther narrative · invaded Greece in 480 BC · married Esther in his 7th year.

entries.name: Artaxerxes I
entries.notes: Authorized Ezra's return in his 7th year (458 BC) · authorized Nehemiah to rebuild the walls in his 20th year (445 BC).

entries.name: Darius II
entries.notes: Last Persian king explicitly named in Scripture.

roman_emperors.title: Roman Emperors of the New Testament
roman_emperors.subtitle: Augustus → Nero · the imperial backdrop of Christ and the early Church

entries.name: Augustus
entries.notes: Decreed the census that brought Joseph and Mary to Bethlehem · first Roman emperor.

entries.name: Tiberius
entries.notes: Reigning emperor when John the Baptist began ministry (15th year, c. AD 28-29) · throughout Christ's public ministry and crucifixion.

entries.name: Caligula
entries.notes: Not named in Scripture · attempted to set up his statue in the Temple.

entries.name: Claudius
entries.notes: Famine in his reign foretold by Agabus · expelled Jews from Rome (Acts 18:2).

entries.name: Nero
entries.notes: The "Caesar" to whom Paul appealed · executed Paul (and tradition holds Peter) · burned Rome in AD 64.

herodian_dynasty.title: The Herodian Dynasty
herodian_dynasty.subtitle: Herod the Great and his successors · 37 BC – AD 100

entries.name: Herod the Great
entries.notes: Edomite by birth · rebuilt the Temple · ordered the slaughter of Bethlehem's infants.

entries.name: Herod Archelaus
entries.notes: Inherited Judea and Samaria · so cruel that Joseph took the family to Galilee instead · deposed by Augustus.

entries.name: Herod Antipas
entries.notes: Tetrarch of Galilee and Perea · beheaded John the Baptist · examined Jesus before His crucifixion · the "that fox" of Luke 13:32.

entries.name: Herod Philip
entries.notes: Tetrarch of Iturea and Trachonitis · rebuilt Caesarea Philippi where Peter confessed Christ (Matt 16:13-16).

entries.name: Herod Agrippa I
entries.notes: Killed James the brother of John · imprisoned Peter · struck dead by an angel for accepting worship (Acts 12:23).

entries.name: Herod Agrippa II
entries.notes: Heard Paul's defense at Caesarea · "almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian" (Acts 26:28).

roman_governors.title: Roman Governors of Judea
roman_governors.subtitle: Pontius Pilate, Felix, Festus · Roman procurators in Acts

entries.name: Pontius Pilate
entries.notes: Fifth Roman prefect of Judea · sentenced Jesus to be crucified · attested in the Pilate Stone inscription found at Caesarea.

entries.name: Felix
entries.notes: Heard Paul · trembled at his preaching · left him bound, hoping for a bribe (Acts 24:25-26).

entries.name: Festus
entries.notes: Successor to Felix · before whom Paul appealed to Caesar · died in office.

high_priests.title: Notable High Priests
high_priests.subtitle: Aaron through Caiaphas
high_priests.source: Various — Exodus, Leviticus, 1–2 Samuel, John, Acts

entries.name: Aaron
entries.notes: First high priest · brother of Moses · tribe of Levi · died on Mount Hor (Num 33:38-39).

entries.name: Eleazar
entries.notes: Aaron's third son and successor.

entries.name: Phinehas
entries.notes: Stopped a plague by zealous action · received the covenant of an everlasting priesthood.

entries.name: Eli
entries.notes: Priest at Shiloh · raised Samuel · died on hearing of the Ark's capture.

entries.name: Zadok
entries.notes: Loyal to David and Solomon · anointed Solomon · ancestor of subsequent legitimate priestly line (Sadducees).

entries.name: Hilkiah
entries.notes: Found the Book of the Law during Josiah's Temple repairs.

entries.name: Joshua
entries.notes: First high priest after the return from exile · co-led the rebuilding of the Temple with Zerubbabel.

entries.name: Annas
entries.notes: Father-in-law of Caiaphas · still called "high priest" though deposed · Jesus first led to him after arrest.

entries.name: Caiaphas
entries.notes: Officiating high priest at Jesus' trial · prophesied that one man would die for the people.

twelve_apostles.title: The Twelve Apostles
twelve_apostles.subtitle: Christ's chosen Twelve · Matthew 10:2–4; Mark 3:16–19; Luke 6:13–16; Acts 1:13

entries.name: Simon Peter
entries.notes: Brother of Andrew · fisherman · spokesman of the Twelve · author of 1 & 2 Peter.

entries.name: Andrew
entries.notes: Brother of Peter · originally a disciple of John the Baptist · brought Peter to Jesus.

entries.name: James (son of Zebedee)
entries.notes: Brother of John · with Peter and John in the inner circle · first apostle martyred (by Herod Agrippa I, c. AD 44).

entries.name: John
entries.notes: "The disciple whom Jesus loved" · author of the Fourth Gospel, three epistles, and Revelation.

entries.name: Philip
entries.notes: From Bethsaida · brought Nathanael to Jesus.

entries.name: Bartholomew
entries.notes: From Cana · Jesus saw him under the fig tree.

entries.name: Thomas
entries.notes: "Doubting Thomas" · would not believe without seeing the wounds · eventually proclaimed "My Lord and my God".

entries.name: Matthew
entries.notes: Tax collector at Capernaum · author of the Gospel of Matthew.

entries.name: James (son of Alphaeus)
entries.notes: Sometimes called "James the Less" · possibly the brother of Matthew.

entries.name: Thaddaeus
entries.notes: Asked Jesus, "Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?"

entries.name: Simon the Zealot
entries.notes: Belonged to (or sympathised with) the Zealot party that opposed Roman rule.

entries.name: Judas Iscariot
entries.notes: Treasurer of the Twelve · betrayed Jesus for thirty pieces of silver · hanged himself.

entries.name: Matthias (replacement)
entries.notes: Chosen by lot to replace Judas after the resurrection · had been with the disciples from John's baptism onward.

entries.name: Paul
entries.notes: Apostle to the Gentiles · former Pharisee and persecutor · author of 13 NT epistles · martyred under Nero, c. AD 67.
